title: gonocarpus confertifolius
intro: Gonocarpus confertifolius, also known as the joint bush or jointed bushy-pea, is a shrub-like plant native to Australia. It is a member of the family Haloragaceae, commonly known as the water-dilloes or coontie family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and wounds. Gonocarpus confertifolius can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion or decoction, and has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from skin irritations.
